---
title: "Fishery Weather Derivative Dashboard"
format: 
  dashboard:
    orientation: rows
---

```{ojs}
//| output: false

// 1. Load data from the same folder to avoid path errors
all_data = FileAttachment("dashboard_data.csv").csv({typed: true})

// 2. Explicitly require D3
d3 = require("d3@7")

priceFloor = 1.0

// 3. Centralized Filtering (Numeric comparison for stability)
// Scenario A logic
data_A_raw = all_data.filter(d => 
  Number(d.wind_threshold) === Number(wind_A) && 
  Number(d.wave_threshold) === Number(wave_A) &&
  d.weather_derivative_price >= priceFloor
)

// Scenario B logic
data_B_raw = all_data.filter(d => 
  Number(d.wind_threshold) === Number(wind_B) && 
  Number(d.wave_threshold) === Number(wave_B) &&
  d.weather_derivative_price >= priceFloor
)

// 4. Shared Rollups (Used by both plots and tables)
data_A_daily = Array.from(d3.rollup(data_A_raw, v => v[0], d => d.contract_day_index).values())
data_B_daily = Array.from(d3.rollup(data_B_raw, v => v[0], d => d.contract_day_index).values())

data_A_pricing = Array.from(d3.rollup(data_A_raw, v => v[0], d => d.strike_percent).values())
data_B_pricing = Array.from(d3.rollup(data_B_raw, v => v[0], d => d.strike_percent).values())
```

## {.sidebar}

### Scenario A (Blue)
```{ojs}
viewof wind_A = Inputs.select([7.5, 8.5, 9.5, 10.5], {value: 8.5, label: "Wind (m/s)"})
viewof wave_A = Inputs.select([1.25, 1.5, 1.75, 2.0], {value: 1.75, label: "Waves (m)"})
```

***

### Scenario B (Orange)
```{ojs}
viewof wind_B = Inputs.select([7.5, 8.5, 9.5, 10.5], {value: 9.5, label: "Wind (m/s)"})
viewof wave_B = Inputs.select([1.25, 1.5, 1.75, 2.0], {value: 1.75, label: "Waves (m)"})
```

## Row {height="60%"}

### Probability Curve A
```{ojs}
// No redundant filtering here, just the plot
Plot.plot({
  y: {grid: true, domain: [0, 1]},
  marks: [
    Plot.areaY(data_A_daily, {x: "contract_day_index", y: "p_fishable", fill: "steelblue", fillOpacity: 0.2}),
    Plot.lineY(data_A_daily, {x: "contract_day_index", y: "p_fishable", stroke: "steelblue", strokeWidth: 2})
  ]
})
```

### Probability Curve B
```{ojs}
Plot.plot({
  y: {grid: true, domain: [0, 1]},
  marks: [
    Plot.areaY(data_B_daily, {x: "contract_day_index", y: "p_fishable", fill: "darkorange", fillOpacity: 0.2}),
    Plot.lineY(data_B_daily, {x: "contract_day_index", y: "p_fishable", stroke: "darkorange", strokeWidth: 2})
  ]
})
```

## Row {height="40%"}

### Financial Table A
```{ojs}
Inputs.table(data_A_pricing, {
  columns: ["strike_percent", "strike_days", "trigger_prob", "weather_derivative_price"],
  header: {
    strike_percent: "Strike %",
    strike_days: "Days",
    trigger_prob: "P(Trigger)",
    weather_derivative_price: "Premium ($)"
  },
  format: {
    strike_percent: d => (d * 100).toFixed(0) + "%",
    trigger_prob: d => d.toFixed(4),
    weather_derivative_price: d => "$" + d.toLocaleString(undefined, {minimumFractionDigits: 2, maximumFractionDigits: 2})
  }
})
```

### Financial Table B
```{ojs}
Inputs.table(data_B_pricing, {
  columns: ["strike_percent", "strike_days", "trigger_prob", "weather_derivative_price"],
  header: {
    strike_percent: "Strike %",
    strike_days: "Days",
    trigger_prob: "P(Trigger)",
    weather_derivative_price: "Premium ($)"
  },
  format: {
    strike_percent: d => (d * 100).toFixed(0) + "%",
    trigger_prob: d => d.toFixed(4),
    weather_derivative_price: d => "$" + d.toLocaleString(undefined, {minimumFractionDigits: 2, maximumFractionDigits: 2})
  }
})
```
